samples.summary: row of 4 colored boxes (overview) — A → B → C → D horizontally
row: children flow A → B → C → D horizontally
column: children flow A → B → C → D vertically
iOS-ext row-reverse: visual order D → C → B → A horizontally (not in JoyDOM spec; covered only by the iOS FlexLayout primitive)
iOS-ext column-reverse: visual order D → C → B → A vertically (not in JoyDOM spec; covered only by the iOS FlexLayout primitive)
flexDirection omitted: falls through to FlexContainerConfig default
interaction: row + flexWrap: wrap with 6 children that overflow to a second line
interaction: column + justifyContent: flex-end packs items at the bottom (main-end)
edge case: container with no children — verifies empty-flex layout doesn't crash
edge case: single child — lays out at the leading edge of the container
interaction: row + alignItems: center with mixed-height children → all centered on cross axis
interaction: row + middle child overrides via alignSelf: flex-end → drops to the bottom
interaction: row + gap: 16px → visible 16px spacing between children
interaction: row + per-child order property → visual sequence differs from declaration order
interaction: row + flexGrow 1/1/2 distributes free space → third child is double-width
interaction: row + flexBasis variants (auto / px / %) → mixed initial sizes
interaction: column + flexWrap: wrap → children overflow vertically and wrap to a second column
responsive flip: column at narrow widths, row at ≥768px (drag viewport slider through 768 to see)
outer row + inner columns → two-tier flex layout, common card-with-sections shape
flexDirection inside a position: absolute child — pinned to relative parent's inset
fixed-width parent (250px) shrinks 3 × 100px children to fit via default flexShrink
flexDirection applied via .class selector on two siblings — both render identically
flexDirection set inline via node.props.style — no selector rule needed
row with flexGrow 1/1/2 — red and green equal width, blue double-width
value sweep: flexGrow: 0 — items stay at their content width, free space at the end
value sweep: flexGrow: 1 on all — equal share of free space (basis: 0 makes the math clean)
value sweep: flexGrow: 2 on all — visually identical to flexGrow: 1 (ratios matter, not absolutes)
value sweep: flexGrow: 0.5 on all — fractional grow factor honored (Double precision)
value sweep: flexGrow 1/1/2 — proportional distribution of free space
flexGrow omitted: defaults to 0 (no growth), free space stays at the main-end
edge case: container with no children — flexGrow has no items to distribute to, layout doesn't crash
edge case: single child with flexGrow: 1 — fills the entire inner content area
edge case: flexGrow 0/1/0 — only the middle child absorbs all free space
flexGrow set inline via node.props.style — no selector rule needed
flexGrow applied via .grow class — all matching siblings split free space equally
context: column direction — flexGrow distributes free space on the vertical main axis
context: 250-wide parent — items with flexGrow: 1 split the inner content space evenly
interaction: flexBasis: 80px + flexGrow 0/1/2 — items start at 80, leftover space distributed proportionally
interaction: width: 120 × 3 in a 300-wide parent → flexShrink kicks in, flexGrow ignored (no free space to distribute)
interaction: row + wrap + flexGrow: 1 — items on each line grow to fill that line's remaining space
interaction: flexGrow: 1 with maxWidth on item a — a clamps at 80px, b and c absorb the freed space
responsive: fixed-width boxes at narrow viewports, flexGrow: 1 + basis: 0 at ≥768px (drag viewport slider through 768 to see)
3 items basis 160 in a 320-wide container — flexShrink 0/1/2 distributes overflow
value sweep: flexShrink: 0 — items keep their 120px width and overflow the 300px container
value sweep: flexShrink: 1 (CSS default) — items shrink equally to fit the container
value sweep: flexShrink: 2 — uniform shrink factor produces identical layout to shrink: 1
value sweep: flexShrink: 0.5 — uniform fractional shrink, same layout as shrink: 1
value sweep: shrink ratios 1/1/2 — blue item shrinks twice as much as red and green
defaults: property omitted — verifies CSS default flexShrink: 1 (items shrink to fit)
edge: empty container — no items to shrink, fixed-size root stays at declared dimensions
edge: single oversized child — shrinks alone from 400px down to the 200px container's inner width
edge: only green (#b) shrinks — red and blue at shrink: 0 absorb all overflow into one item
authoring: flexShrink declared inline via node.props.style — ratios 0/1/2
authoring: flexShrink declared via .fixed/.shrink-1/.shrink-2 class selectors
context: flexDirection: column — vertical shrink along main axis when stack overflows height
nested flex containers — outer shrink fits inner container into available width, then inner shrink fits its own children
context: narrow 220px container forces heavier shrink than the standard 300px overview
interaction: shrink: 1 with basis 80/160/240 — CSS weighted shrink (basis x shrink) hits larger items harder
interaction: shrink + grow both 1, container undersized — shrink wins when content overflows
interaction: flexWrap: wrap defeats shrink — items wrap to new lines at natural size instead of shrinking
interaction: minWidth: 100 on red clamps its shrink — remaining overflow redistributes to green and blue
responsive: shrink: 1 at narrow viewports (items shrink to fit), shrink: 0 at >=768px (items keep 120px width)
row with explicit flexBasis (60px / 120px / 30%) — initial sizes before flex
sweep: flexBasis 0 + flexGrow 1 — free space divided equally across 3 items
sweep: flexBasis: auto — items use their declared width (80px) as basis
sweep: flexBasis: 80px — fixed px basis on each item, free space left at end
sweep: flexBasis: 140px + flexShrink: 0 — large fixed basis fills most of the container
sweep: flexBasis: 50% — each item gets half the container main-axis size
default: flexBasis omitted (CSS-default auto) — items use width: 90px as basis
edge: empty flex container — no children, no items to size
edge: single child with flexBasis 200px — basis honored without inter-item interaction
edge: mixed units auto + px + % — each basis resolved against its own value/unit
edge: nested flex container — outer basis 240px, inner items basis 50% resolve against the outer's main-axis content size
authoring: inline node.props.style — each item declares its own flexBasis directly
authoring: .box class selector — shared flexBasis applied via class rule
context: column direction — flexBasis resolves against the cross-axis-perpendicular (height) main axis
context: fixed-width 300 container — % basis (30%) resolves against the container's main-axis width
interaction: flexBasis: 60 + flexGrow: 1 — items start at 60 then divide leftover free space equally
interaction: flexBasis: 140 + flexShrink: 1 in a 300 container — items shrink uniformly from their basis to fit
interaction: flexBasis 120 + flexWrap: wrap + shrink 0 — items keep their basis width and overflow onto next line
interaction: flexBasis with minWidth/maxWidth clamps — red 40px basis clamps up to 80, green 200px basis clamps down to 120
responsive: basis 90px at narrow viewports, basis 0 + grow 1 at >=768px (items stretch to fill)
row + justifyContent: space-between — boxes pinned to start, middle, end
value sweep: flex-start packs items at the main-axis start
value sweep: flex-end packs items at the main-axis end
value sweep: center packs items in the middle of the main axis
value sweep: space-between pins first/last to edges, distributes equal gaps between items
value sweep: space-around — each item has equal space on both sides (edges get half-gap)
value sweep: space-evenly — equal gaps including before first and after last item
default: justifyContent omitted — CSS spec fallback is flex-start
edge: empty container with space-between — no items to distribute
edge: single child with center — only positioning matters (no distribution between items)
edge: two items with space-around — illustrates space-around vs space-between divergence at low item count
authoring: justifyContent declared inline via node.props.style on root
authoring: justifyContent applied via .row-center class selector on the root container
context: column direction — justifyContent now operates on the vertical (main) axis
context: narrower fixed-width container — less free space, space-between gaps shrink proportionally
interaction: with flexGrow:1 on items — no free space remains, justifyContent has no visible effect
interaction: flexWrap:wrap — justifyContent applies per-line to remaining free space on each row
interaction: gap consumed first, then center distributes remaining free space
interaction: flexBasis 20% items — space-between distributes the remaining 40% across the gaps
edge: nested flex containers — outer uses flex-end, inner uses space-between (independent main-axis distribution)
responsive: flex-start at narrow viewport; >=768px breakpoint flips to center
row + alignItems: center with 3 different-height boxes (40/70/100) — all vertically centered
row + alignItems: flex-start — 60x60 boxes pinned to the top of a 140px-tall container
row + alignItems: flex-end — 60x60 boxes pinned to the bottom of a 140px-tall container
row + alignItems: center — 60x60 boxes vertically centered in a 140px-tall container
row + alignItems: stretch — boxes with no explicit height stretch to fill the cross axis
default: alignItems omitted — verifies the CSS-spec fallback of stretch
edge: empty flex container with alignItems: center — renders the bare root background
edge: single 60x60 child with alignItems: center — child vertically centered
edge: 3 boxes of mixed heights (40/70/100) with alignItems: center — each centered on its cross axis
authoring: alignItems declared inline via node.props.style on the root container
authoring: alignItems declared via class selector (.row-center) on the root container
context: flexDirection column + alignItems: center — cross axis is horizontal, boxes centered left-to-right
context: fixed-width root (280px) + alignItems: flex-end — boxes pinned to bottom regardless of available main-axis room
interaction: flexWrap + alignItems: flex-start with mixed-height children — within each line, items align to the line's top edge
interaction: gap: 24 + alignItems: center — main-axis gap doesn't disturb cross-axis centering
interaction: flexGrow: 1 + alignItems: flex-end — main-axis growth doesn't disturb cross-axis flex-end pinning
interaction: alignItems: flex-start container, middle child overrides with alignSelf: flex-end — only that child drops to the bottom
responsive: narrow viewport renders alignItems: flex-start; ≥768px breakpoint flips to flex-end (wide variant captured by testAlignItemsResponsiveWide)
iOS-ext baseline: 3 text nodes of different font sizes align on their text baselines (not in JoyDOM spec; covered only by the iOS FlexLayout primitive)
row, middle child has alignSelf: flex-end — drops to the bottom of the container
alignSelf: auto on middle child inherits container's alignItems: flex-end
alignSelf: flex-start on middle child overrides container's alignItems: flex-end
alignSelf: flex-end on middle child overrides container's alignItems: flex-start
alignSelf: center on middle child overrides container's alignItems: flex-start
alignSelf: stretch on middle child (no height) expands to fill cross axis
alignSelf omitted — children inherit container's alignItems: center
edge: empty container, no children
edge: single child overrides container alignItems via alignSelf: flex-start
edge: every child sets a different alignSelf — sweep along the cross axis
authoring: alignSelf declared via inline node.props.style
authoring: alignSelf via .drop class shared between selectors
context: column container — alignSelf shifts middle child along the horizontal cross axis
context: tall fixed-height container exposes alignSelf center vs flex-end positions
interaction: alignItems: stretch parent — one child with fixed height opts into alignSelf: center
interaction: alignItems: flex-end parent — two siblings override to flex-start and center
interaction: flexWrap: wrap — alignSelf: flex-end shifts shorter items to the bottom of each flex line
interaction: children of varying heights, some override container alignItems
responsive: alignSelf: flex-start at narrow flips to auto (inherit flex-end) at ≥768px
iOS-ext baseline: alignSelf: baseline (not in JoyDOM spec; covered only by the iOS FlexLayout primitive)
row + flexWrap: wrap — 6 boxes overflow to a second line
nowrap: 3 boxes wider than container shrink to one line (default behavior, explicit)
wrap: 3 boxes overflow container width and reflow onto two lines
default (flexWrap omitted): items shrink onto one line, verifying nowrap fallback
wrap container with zero children — renders as empty 320x80 background
wrap with one child: no wrapping needed, single box at start
wrap but children fit — stays on one line, no wrapping triggered
flexWrap declared via node.props.style on the root container (inline authoring)
flexWrap supplied via a .wrap-row class selector on the root container
column + wrap: 4 boxes overflow fixed height and start a new column
wrap inside a nested fixed-width 200px container — wrap triggered by the nested width
wrap + per-axis gap (c:16, r:24) — row gap visibly separates wrapped lines
wrap + justifyContent: space-between — each line distributes its own free space
wrap + flexGrow: 1 — each wrapped line's items grow to fill that line's free space
wrap + flexShrink: 0 — items keep declared width, overflow wraps rather than shrinks
responsive: narrow viewport wraps, ≥768px breakpoint flips to nowrap (narrow canvas)
iOS-ext wrap-reverse: wrap with cross axis reversed — first line at bottom (not in JoyDOM spec; covered only by the iOS FlexLayout primitive)
row + gap: 24px — visible 24-pt spacing between boxes
gap: 0 — boxes touch with no spacing
gap: 4px — thin spacing between row items
gap: 16px — medium spacing between row items
gap: 32px — wide spacing between row items
gap omitted — verifies CSS-spec default of 0px (boxes touch)
edge case: container with no children + gap declared — should not crash
edge case: single child — no inter-item gap is produced
two items with gap: 16 — exactly one gap rendered
row + wrap + rowGap: 16 — only vertical spacing between wrapped rows
column direction + rowGap: 16 — vertical spacing between stacked items
row direction + columnGap: 16 — horizontal spacing between row items
column direction + columnGap: 16 — no effect (no second column)
gap: 16 + rowGap: 4 — rowGap overrides vertical, columnGap inherits 16
gap: 16 + columnGap: 4 — columnGap overrides horizontal, rowGap inherits 16
gap declared inline via node style — same visual as class-selector
gap on container, colors via class selectors — authoring-style variant
column direction + gap: 16 — vertical spacing between stacked items
fixed-width container + gap: 12 — gap consumes free space deterministically
row + wrap + rowGap: 12 + columnGap: 8 — both axes visible across 2x2 grid
flexGrow: 1 children + gap: 16 — gap reserved before growth distribution
justifyContent: center + gap: 16 — gap survives main-axis centering
responsive: gap: 4 at narrow, gap: 32 at ≥768px breakpoint
3 boxes declared a/b/c with order 3/1/2 — visual order is b → c → a
value sweep: order: 0 on all items — source order preserved (red, green, blue)
value sweep: orders 3/2/1 on three items — reverses source order (blue, green, red)
value sweep: orders 1/0/-1 — negative renders first (blue, green, red)
value sweep: orders 0/-1/-2 — all-negative order chain renders most-negative first (blue, green, red)
value sweep: orders 1000/10/100 — only relative magnitude matters (green, blue, red)
default (omitted): all items use order 0 — source order renders unchanged
edge: empty container — no children to reorder
edge: single child with order: 5 — ordering is a no-op
edge: all four items share order: 1 — source order preserved (CSS-spec stable)
authoring: order set via inline node.props.style on each child
authoring: order applied via .first/.second/.third class selectors
context: flexDirection column — order reorders vertically (green, blue, red top-to-bottom)
context: fixed-width 300px container — ordering does not affect sizing (blue, red, green)
interaction: order + justifyContent: space-between — reordered children get distributed (green, blue, red)
interaction: order + flexWrap: wrap — ordered sequence (green, amber, blue, red) flows into two rows
interaction: order + flexGrow: 1 — items reorder then grow to fill (green, blue, red)
interaction: order combined with per-item alignSelf — reordering preserves each item's cross-axis alignment
responsive: source order at narrow viewports, order 3/1/2 reorder at ≥768px (drag viewport slider through 768 to see)
overview: 3 in-flow boxes plus an absolutely-positioned amber pin anchored to the root
absolute: middle (green) box removed from flow and anchored at top:40, left:120; red and blue close the gap
relative: all three boxes stay in flow at default offsets; layout identical to default flow
default: position omitted — boxes flow normally (CSS default = relative)
edge case: positioned container with no children renders without crashing
edge case: single absolute child anchored to its positioned parent
edge case: absolute child with no positioned ancestor — anchors to the root viewport
authoring: position declared inline via node.props.style (no class selectors)
authoring: position applied via .pinned class selector — blue box detaches to top-right
context: absolute child inside a row flex container — removed from flow, siblings collapse the gap
context: two absolute children anchored to opposite corners of a fixed-width parent
interaction: absolute child positioned with top:20 and left:40 insets relative to its parent
interaction: two absolute siblings stacked by zIndex (blue z:1 in front of red z:0)
interaction: absolute child positioned beyond parent's right edge — clipped by overflow:hidden
interaction: deeply nested absolute child anchors to the closest positioned ancestor (inner), not root
responsive: middle box is relative at narrow viewports; at >=768px it switches to absolute and detaches
iOS-ext fixed: position:'fixed' falls back to absolute on iOS with a warning diagnostic (not in JoyDOM spec)
iOS-ext sticky: position:'sticky' falls back to absolute on iOS with a warning diagnostic (not in JoyDOM spec)
flex container plus a hidden node via display:none
display:flex on root lays children out in a row
display:none on middle child removes it from flow; siblings reflow with gap collapsing
display omitted entirely; joydom-swift defaults to flex layout
display:flex container with no children renders an empty box at its fixed size
single child in a display:flex container renders normally
display:none on the middle of three siblings — first and third reflow together
display:none on a flex container hides the container and all descendants
display declared inline via node.props.style — middle child set to none
display applied via class selectors (.shown / .gone) — middle child hidden
display:none child inside a nested flex row container; outer + inner survive
display:none inside a fixed-width container — hidden child contributes zero space
display:none child doesn't claim flex-grow share; remaining siblings split the space
display:none child in a wrapping container — wrapping computed over visible items only
display:none child contributes zero space — container padding/gap unaffected by it
responsive: at narrow viewport b is visible; at >=768px b becomes display:none
iOS-ext block: display:block on a flex child (not in JoyDOM spec; covered only by the iOS implementation)
iOS-ext inline: display:inline on a flex child (not in JoyDOM spec; covered only by the iOS implementation)
iOS-ext inline-block: display:inline-block on a flex child (not in JoyDOM spec; covered only by the iOS implementation)
iOS-ext inline-flex: display:inline-flex on a nested container (not in JoyDOM spec; covered only by the iOS implementation)
side-by-side: border-box vs content-box cards with identical declared width/padding/border so the deduction is unambiguous
border-box mode: outer 120 with padding 16 + border 4 → content area 80 fits an 80x80 inner exactly
content-box default (boxSizing omitted): width 120 is the content area; outer expands to 160 with padding+border
border-box width 120 vs content-box width 80 (both +padding 16, border 4) yield the same outer 120 box
no padding or border: border-box and content-box render identically — nothing to deduct
width 100, padding 16: border-box content area = 68; content-box content area = 100 (outer = 132)
width 100, padding 16, border 4: border-box content area = 60; content-box content area = 100 (outer = 140)
border-box card with no children — bounds are still set by the box-sizing deduction
single child sized exactly to the border-box content area (outer 140 - padding 32 - border 8 = 100)
boxSizing declared via node.props.style (inline) — same deduction as class-based
boxSizing applied via .border-box class selector composed onto a .card
three cards on a flex row sized via flexBasis 120 — boxSizing alternates and changes content area without changing outer
fixed 380px container, two width-140 cards: border-box fits in 140, content-box swells to 180
flexGrow distributes free space over the final flex base size — boxSizing affects starting base but grow distribution is symmetric
flexShrink against a 300px container: border-box and content-box cards shrink from different effective starting widths
border-box with minWidth (clamps up) and maxWidth (clamps down) — clamp is applied to the outer (border-box) size
three nested border-box containers (outer 200x160, middle 160x120, inner 120x80) — deduction applies at every level independently
narrow viewport renders as content-box (default); width>=768px breakpoint flips to border-box — same JSON, different outer size
Three overlapping absolutely-positioned tiles with mixed zIndex stacking (red:1, blue:2, green:3 — green on top)
Two overlapping positioned siblings both at zIndex 0 — later sibling (green) paints on top
Two overlapping positioned siblings: red z:1, green z:2 — green renders on top
Negative zIndex pushes element behind sibling: green z:-1 sits behind red z:0
Four overlapping tiles stacked by zIndex -1, 0, 1, 2 (red behind, amber on top)
Large zIndex values (10, 100, 1000) — only relative ordering matters; magnitude is irrelevant
Default (omitted) zIndex on overlapping positioned siblings — equivalent to 0/0, later sibling on top
Empty root with zIndex declared — verifies decode and no children to stack
Single positioned child with zIndex — nothing to stack against
Three in-flow flex items with zIndex declared but no position — per CSS spec, zIndex has no effect on non-positioned elements
Inline node.props.style authoring of zIndex on overlapping positioned siblings
zIndex applied via class selectors (.behind / .front) on positioned tiles
zIndex on tiles whose containing block is itself an absolutely-positioned parent
zIndex on tiles inside a relatively-positioned card (typical containing block setup)
High zIndex tile (z:999) still clips at parent's overflow:hidden boundary — zIndex doesn't escape clipping
Top tile (z:2) with opacity 0.6 — red below shows through green
Three tiles all at zIndex:1 — equal zIndex falls back to source order (blue paints last on top)
Responsive zIndex flip: narrow green z:1 (on top), wide (>=768px) green z:-1 (behind)
Four-cell showcase: visible, hidden, scroll, auto each wrapping an oversized child
overflow: visible — oversized child spills outside the 160x100 container, fully visible
overflow: hidden — oversized child clipped at the 160x100 container edge
overflow: clip — semantically no scroll container; expected to render identical to hidden
overflow: scroll — wraps content in a ScrollView; in a static snapshot content is clipped at scroll bounds
overflow: auto — ViewThatFits switches to a scrolling variant when content overflows
overflow omitted — verifies CSS default 'visible' (oversized child spills)
empty container with overflow: hidden — paints the container box only
single child smaller than container — overflow setting has no observable effect
single oversized child clipped by overflow: hidden on a 160x100 container
overflow: hidden declared inline via node.props.style
overflow: hidden applied via a .clipped class selector
Two siblings in a flex row: clipper (overflow:hidden) vs bleeder (overflow:visible) with identical oversized children
overflow: hidden on a fixed-width AND fixed-height container — child clipped on both axes
absolutely-positioned child extending beyond the relative parent with overflow: hidden — verifies clip applies to abs descendants
overflow: hidden + borderRadius — verifies whether the clip rect follows the rounded corners
overflow: hidden with large container padding — clip happens at the container outer edge
Two nested overflow:hidden containers — innermost child clipped by inner, inner clipped by outer
narrow viewport: overflow: hidden; >=480px breakpoint flips to overflow: visible
Absolutely-positioned child pinned to all four edges of its frame
top: 30px on an absolute child — vertical offset from relative parent's top edge
left: 40px on an absolute child — horizontal offset from relative parent's left edge
bottom: 20px on an absolute child — anchored 20px above the relative parent's bottom edge
right: 24px on an absolute child — anchored 24px left of the relative parent's right edge
top + left combined — anchors absolute child to a top-left corner offset
bottom + right combined — anchors absolute child to a bottom-right corner offset
All four insets on a width-less/height-less absolute child — stretches to fill the inset rectangle
default: no insets declared on an absolute child — anchors at the in-flow position (top-left of content box, after padding)
Empty container — verifies an insets-capable parent renders with no children without error
top: 0, left: 0 — pins absolute child to the top-left corner exactly
top: -10, left: -10 — element shifts outside the relative parent's box (no clip)
Insets declared inline via node.props.style (authoring sweep)
Insets declared via class selector .pinned-tl (authoring sweep)
Absolute child anchors to the nearest positioned (relative) ancestor — a nested frame inside root
Absolute child anchors to an absolutely-positioned ancestor frame
Absolute child with NO positioned ancestor — anchors to the initial containing block (root)
Two absolute children with overlapping offsets — z-index determines paint order
Absolute child with negative insets inside a relative+overflow:hidden frame — parent clips off-canvas portion

position: relative + top/left — DOCUMENTED LIMITATION: JoyDOM-swift only applies insets when position: absolute; relative items render at flow position (insets currently no-op). CSS spec would shift the green box down 20 / right 10.
responsive: top:0/left:0 narrow → top:40/left:80 at ≥768px (drag viewport slider through 768 to see)

All five values stacked: fill, contain, cover, none, scale-down
fill: stretch landscape image into a 240x160 box, ignoring aspect ratio
contain: portrait image letterboxed inside landscape box (aspect preserved, no crop)
cover: portrait image cropped to fill landscape box (aspect preserved, edges cropped)
none: landscape image at intrinsic 400x200 size inside a 240x160 box (overflow clipped)
scale-down: portrait image scaled to fit (matches contain for this intrinsic-larger case)
default (objectFit omitted): falls back to fill — landscape image stretched to box

interaction: cover + objectPosition top-right (position is a no-op for cover at exact aspect crop)
responsive: contain on narrow viewport, cover at width>=768px (recorded at narrow)
